# CMSNext Repository Memory

Use this skill when a task needs the compact, high-signal version of repository conventions before implementing or reviewing changes.

## When To Use
- You need architecture reminders before editing services, hooks, or storage code.
- You want the repo's naming, logging, or storage rules in one place.
- You are reviewing code and want the standard pitfalls to check first.
- You need a concise index before loading deeper docs.

## Procedure
1. Start with the repository memory reference.
2. If the task touches a specific layer, load the matching guide from `.github/`.
3. Apply the rules as constraints while implementing or reviewing.
4. Use the repo-level validation commands before finishing substantial work.
